stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant release in the LLM space.
It is tracked via Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its relevance within the ML community.
The associated pipeline is text-generation, confirming its primary application as a generative AI model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and practical use of Meta's Llama architecture.

stories.title: Anthropic got hit by export rules nobody understands
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic was forced to restrict access to its latest AI models (Fable 5 and Mythos 5) due to new export rules.
The restrictions, reportedly from the Trump administration, abruptly blocked access for all foreign nationals, including some US users and employees.
The situation highlights the growing regulatory and geopolitical hurdles facing advanced AI development.

stories.title: AI coding agents can autonomously direct robot training
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: NVIDIA is integrating AI coding agents into its robotic self-improvement programs.
These agents autonomously direct the training processes for robots.
This represents a significant step toward more autonomous and self-optimizing robotic systems.

stories.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
stories.author: Lily Hay Newman, WIRED.com
bullet_points: Advanced AI models are predicted to soon possess significant hacking capabilities.
The proliferation of these 'dangerous' AI models is presented as an inevitable trend.
The story signals a major shift in cybersecurity risks due to AI advancement.

stories.title: Looped World Models
stories.author: Wai Lam
bullet_points: Introduces Looped World Models (LoopWM), a novel architecture addressing the tension between simulation fidelity and computational cost in world models.
LoopWM uses an iterative refinement process via a parameter-shared transformer block to improve latent environment state representation.
Achieves up to 100x parameter efficiency compared to conventional world modeling approaches.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline shown is for text-generation, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance on Apple Silicon.
The integration leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for optimization.
This is presented as a preview, indicating an ongoing development and performance enhancement.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a new personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
Its key feature is connecting these apps to local AI coding agents.
The entire system is designed to run locally on the user's own device, emphasizing privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Epic wants to let you bring your Fortnite skins to other games
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Epic Games is moving toward a more interoperable metaverse vision.
The initiative is being enabled by the release of Unreal Engine 6.
The core feature allows developers to create games that can utilize assets like Fortnite skins across different titles.

stories.title: EvolveNav: Proactive Preflection and Self-Evolving Memory for Zero-Shot Object Goal Navigation
stories.author: Hao Wang
bullet_points: Introduces EvolveNav, a framework for Zero-Shot Object-Goal Navigation (ZS-OGN) that allows agents to learn without prior training.
Addresses the limitation of existing foundation models by incorporating a self-evolving memory for continuous test-time improvement.
The core innovation involves building an 'agentic rule memory' by extracting actionable knowledge from the navigation process.

stories.title: A Red-Team Study of Anthropic Fable 5 & Opus 4.8 Models
stories.author: Nicola Franco
bullet_points: The study performs a red-team evaluation of Anthropic's frontier LLMs, Fable 5 and Opus 4.8.
It tests the models' adversarial robustness against four families of automated jailbreak attacks across 7,826 harmful intents.
The methodology uses the HackAgent framework and employs a panel of three judge models for independent adjudication of attack successes.

stories.title: The Stanford EDGAR Filings Dataset: Reconstructing U.S. Corporate and Financial Disclosures into Layout-Faithful and Token-Efficient Pretraining Data
stories.author: Kay Giesecke
bullet_points: Introduces the Stanford EDGAR Filings Dataset (SEFD), an open-source reconstruction of SEC filings.
Addresses the scarcity of high-quality, clean, long-context documents needed for training Large Language Models (LLMs).
Formats the data into layout-faithful MultiMarkdown specifically for financial language model pretraining.

stories.title: Looped World Models
stories.author: Wai Lam
bullet_points: Introduces Looped World Models (LoopWM), a novel architecture addressing the trade-off between simulation fidelity and computational cost in world modeling.
LoopWM uses an iterative refinement process via a parameter-shared transformer block to improve latent environment state accuracy.
Achieves up to 100x parameter efficiency compared to conventional world modeling approaches.

stories.title: Looped World Models
stories.author: Wai Lam
bullet_points: Introduces Looped World Models (LoopWM), a novel looped architecture for world modeling.
Addresses the trade-off between deep simulation fidelity and computational cost in current world models.
Achieves up to 100x parameter efficiency by iteratively refining latent states using a parameter-shared transformer block.

stories.title: A Red-Team Study of Anthropic Fable 5 & Opus 4.8 Models
stories.author: Nicola Franco
bullet_points: The study performs a red-teaming evaluation on Anthropic's frontier LLMs (Fable 5 and Opus 4.8).
It tests adversarial robustness against four families of automated jailbreak attacks across 7,826 harmful intents.
The methodology uses the HackAgent framework and employs a panel of judge models for independent adjudication of attack successes.

stories.title: The hacker sent by Anthropic to calm the government's nerves about AI safety
stories.author: Brajeshwar
bullet_points: Anthropic is employing a specific researcher (Nicholas Carlini) in a role related to AI safety.
The deployment of this researcher is framed as an effort to reassure government bodies regarding AI safety concerns.
The story highlights the ongoing tension and focus on safety protocols within leading AI development labs.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has significantly improved performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The update results in faster response times and lower memory consumption for LLMs.
Model outputs are noted to be of higher quality following the optimization.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically benefiting users on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: A Pew Research poll indicates a significant public concern, with 63% of Americans believing AI technology is advancing too rapidly.
AI chatbot usage is growing substantially, increasing from 33% in 2024 to a reported 49% currently.
ChatGPT usage specifically has doubled since 2023, highlighting rapid adoption of generative AI tools.

stories.title: Visual Verification Enables Inference-time Steering and Autonomous Policy Improvement
stories.author: Dhruv Shah
bullet_points: Introduces VERITAS, a generator-verifier framework designed for generalist robot policies.
The framework allows for inference-time policy steering and autonomous self-improvement by learning from real-world experience.
It pairs a pre-trained generalist policy ('generator') with a gradient-free 'visual verifier' to evaluate actions dynamically.

stories.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
stories.author: Ameet Talwalkar
bullet_points: Introduces ReproRepo, a new framework designed to scale the evaluation of research reproducibility.
Addresses the major bottleneck in current reproducibility benchmarks: the high manual effort required for data curation.
Leverages naturally occurring human-raised GitHub issues to provide realistic supervision for reproduction audits.

stories.title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
stories.author: Xenofon Koutsoukos
bullet_points: The research focuses on developing autonomous cyber-defense agents using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to combat sophisticated cyber-attacks.
A key innovation is the integration of neurosymbolic approaches, specifically behavior trees with learning-enabled components (LECs), for reasoning and adaptation.
The study addresses the challenge of partially observable systems in cybersecurity, where the attacker's actions are not fully known.

stories.title: RubricsTree: Scalable and Evolving Open-Ended Evaluation of Personal Health Agents across Health Memory and Medical Skills
stories.author: Ahmed A. Metwally
bullet_points: Addresses the critical bottleneck of open-ended evaluation for LLM-powered personal health agents.
Introduces RubricsTree, a new scalable framework to overcome the limitations of costly human annotation and subjective LLM-as-a-judge methods.
Focuses on improving the reliability and scalability of evaluating health agents across health memory and medical skills.

stories.title: DRFLOW: A Deep Research Benchmark for Personalized Workflow Prediction
stories.author: Issam H. Laradji
bullet_points: Introduces DRFLOW, a new benchmark designed for Deep Research (DR) systems.
Addresses a gap in current DR research, which focuses on summaries rather than concrete, actionable workflows.
Aims to enable AI agents to determine step-by-step processes for complex enterprise tasks (e.g., headcount requests).

stories.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
stories.author: Ameet Talwalkar
bullet_points: Introduces ReproRepo, a new framework designed to scale the evaluation of research reproducibility.
Addresses the scalability bottleneck in current reproducibility benchmarks, which require significant manual effort.
Leverages human-raised GitHub issues as a natural supervision signal for realistic reproduction testing.

stories.title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
stories.author: Xenofon Koutsoukos
bullet_points: The research focuses on developing autonomous cyber-defense agents using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to combat sophisticated cyber-attacks.
A key innovation is the integration of neurosymbolic approaches, specifically behavior trees with learning-enabled components (LECs), for reasoning and adaptation.
The study addresses the challenge of partially observable systems in cybersecurity, where the attacker's actions are not fully known.

stories.title: Multi-Source Cybersecurity Logs: An ATT&CK-Labeled Dataset and SLM Evaluation
stories.author: Md Rayhanur Rahman
bullet_points: The paper introduces ATLAS, a new dataset designed to capture multi-stage cyberattacks by correlating system, network, and browser logs.
A key challenge addressed is the limitation of existing public datasets, which often focus on only one type of log (e.g., network-only or host-focused).
The work aims to advance machine learning detection capabilities for complex cyber threats by providing necessary labeled, multi-source data for SLM evaluation.

stories.title: Kolmogorov Regression for Robust Diffusion Policies
stories.author: Lekan Molu
bullet_points: Addresses temporal drift in finite-dimensional diffusion policies caused by discretization artifacts.
Introduces a backward Kolmogorov equation to lift diffusion policies into a Cameron-Martin space.
Replaces stochastic score matching with a deterministic boundary-value Partial Differential Equation (PDE) problem.

stories.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
stories.author: Ameet Talwalkar
bullet_points: ReproRepo is a new framework designed to scale the evaluation of research reproducibility.
It addresses the scalability issue in existing reproducibility benchmarks by using human-raised GitHub issues for supervision.
The framework aims to make it easier to test how well LLM agents can assist in reproducing scientific research results.

stories.title: RubricsTree: Scalable and Evolving Open-Ended Evaluation of Personal Health Agents across Health Memory and Medical Skills
stories.author: Ahmed A. Metwally
bullet_points: Introduces RubricsTree, a new scalable evaluation framework for personal health agents.
Addresses the critical bottleneck in deploying health AI: the lack of scalable, objective evaluation methods.
Compares the limitations of traditional physician annotation (costly) versus LLM-as-a-judge methods (subjective).

stories.title: Learning from the Self-future: On-policy Self-distillation for dLLMs
stories.author: Shiwei Liu
bullet_points: Introduces d-OPSD, the first On-policy Self-distillation (OPSD) framework specifically designed for diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap by adapting OPSD, which is traditionally autoregressive-centric, to handle the arbitrary-order generation of dLLMs.
The research tackles the conflict between standard token-level divergence supervision and the non-sequential nature of diffusion models.

stories.title: The Measurement Gap in the Automation of EU Law: Benchmarking Doctrinal Legal Reasoning under the EU AI Act
stories.author: Michèle Finck
bullet_points: Current LLMs can generate legally competent text, but existing benchmarks fail to test core doctrinal legal reasoning.
The research highlights a 'measurement gap' in evaluating AI's ability to perform complex legal interpretation, not just basic tasks.
This gap is critical because the EU AI Act mandates 'appropriate accuracy' for high-risk AI in judicial settings.

stories.title: Launch HN: Adam (YC W25) – Open-Source AI CAD
stories.author: zachdive
bullet_points: Launch of Adam, an open-source Text-to-CAD platform.
The core premise is using AI agents to generate mechanical designs directly from text.
The approach emphasizes generating CAD as code, mirroring modern software development paradigms.

stories.title: How we run Firecracker VMs inside EC2 and start browsers in less than 1s
stories.author: gregpr07
bullet_points: Focuses on optimizing Virtual Machine (VM) performance using Firecracker within the AWS EC2 environment.
Highlights a significant performance achievement: launching browsers in under one second.
Addresses infrastructure efficiency and speed, which is relevant to cloud computing and serverless architectures.

stories.title: US holds off blacklisting DeepSeek, more than 100 firms deemed security risks
stories.author: giuliomagnifico
bullet_points: The US government is reviewing security risks associated with AI firms, specifically mentioning DeepSeek.
Over 100 firms have been identified as potential security risks by US authorities.
The news indicates ongoing regulatory scrutiny and geopolitical concerns surrounding AI technology.

stories.title: The rsync disaster proves AI isn't ready for infrastructure code
stories.author: adioof
bullet_points: The article discusses a real-world failure of AI (Claude) when used to manage critical infrastructure code (rsync).
It highlights the current limitations of AI in handling complex, low-level systems programming.
The incident serves as a cautionary tale regarding the readiness of AI for production infrastructure tasks.

stories.title: The Context Tax: Why Step 12 Costs 42x Step 1 (Measure It in 40 Lines)
stories.author: alex_spinov
bullet_points: The article discusses a performance/cost inefficiency in AI agent workflows.
The core problem is that each agent step re-sends the entire transcript as input.
This inefficiency was quantified by a 42.8x re-bill multiplier in a debugging session.
